reasoning: This is by design. `is_empty` means "no user-space mappings" — kernel entries below `kernel_l4_end` are shared across all page tables and are supposed to persist even when a table is "empty." The invariant `kernel_entries@ =~= self.kernel_entries_ghost@` ensures kernel entries are consistent, not absent.

The precondition explicitly requires `new_mm.page_tables =~= old_mm.page_tables`, so identical PCID mappings follow trivially from structural equality. This is a tautological consequence of the preconditions, not a spec gap.

The preconditions state `!old_mm.ioid_active(ret)` (ret was free) and `new_mm.iommu_tables =~= old_mm.iommu_tables` (tables unchanged). Since `iommutables_wf` requires free IOIDs to have `is_empty()` tables (which includes empty mappings), and `new_mm.wf()` holds, the empty mapping follows correctly from the free-implies-empty invariant.
